message: How am I supposed to search in such a vast place?
If they’re filming, they should be noticeable.
It's my first time seeing the film crew up close.
Hey, you’re late; I was waiting with my neck stretched out.
No need to worry about it. I wasn’t just sitting around without doing anything.
You said you were free, but you sure look busy. So, I hear you're working on Meryl’s request?
It's Meryl. I don’t know what she requested, though.
I thought it was Yuriko spreading the word.
By the way, what did Meryl request?
In this world, it’s the rule not to reveal the client's secrets.
Yeah, you’re right. Just like in dramas and movies, as expected of a detective.
I can’t tell if I’m being complimented or insulted.
Please contact me if you find Cindy. The phone number is 378-9646. We can discuss rewards then.
Ignoring my controlling voice, she strode away.
Have we come to an agreement?
Do your best, detective. It's no exaggeration to say that the fate of this movie rests on your shoulders.
The location shoot is still in progress. It's my first time encountering a scene like this, so I’m a bit excited.
It's from situations like this that movies are made.
The director seems to be as energetic as ever. He might be the type who gets pumped up when shooting starts.
He has a gentle smile, but I've heard he can be scary during filming.
He might be wearing a gloomy expression because Cindy is gone.
But this might be his serious face.
With his eyes narrowed more tightly, he looks angry.
He seems to dislike me, but is that why he’s making such a sour face?
By the way, what was your request?
well, it’s quicker to just talk to the person directly. Hey, Gloria!
So, Gloria, make sure you take care of it.
Um, what kind of matter is it?
Just remember, I never thought I’d ask you for something.
Then you don’t have to force me; I wasn't plotting to get work from the director.
Even if I’m not inclined, I can't help but follow the director's orders.
Huh, is that how it works?
Anyway, that’s why you should go find Cindy.
Are you hard of hearing? I said to look for Cindy. That's the request.
I wonder if you'll actually be able to find her?
That's why I put out the request, right?
I really think she's hiding somewhere in New York, but I just can't figure out where she is.
I think she looks really different without her usual face. She looks much younger.
He's a skilled detective, so he should be able to find her right away.
Does that mean the director passed the job to you?
Whatever his intentions are, I'm sure he's seen as the guy who got work from Gloria thanks to the director's favor.
He must've wanted to be involved with detectives too.
That same sulky face from the party.
It makes me feel like he did something bad.
You'd think he'd be more frazzled if Cindy's gone, but—
Could it be that he has some idea of where Cindy went?
Either way, this just gave me another job.
From Gloria's reaction, I can't imagine she was kidnapped; it feels more like she left of her own accord.
Is it possible she went missing because the work was too tough, wanting to return to being a normal girl?
It's not like she wanted to run away from Gloria, right?
